question: A 37-year-old woman died in her sleep. There is no apparent trauma. Microscopy of the left circumflex artery is shown. Which underlying condition is she most likely to have?
A 72-year-old woman dies of congestive heart failure. The aortic valve is examined at autopsy. What is the most likely cause of aortic stenosis in this patient?

A 30-year-old woman has a heart murmur, recurrent arthritis, skin rash, glomerulonephritis, negative blood cultures, positive ANA, and positive anti-dsDNA. What is the most likely cause of the murmur?

A 16-year-old girl from Africa presents with chest pain, respiratory distress, pansystolic murmur, S3, marked cardiomegaly, and myocardial mononuclear cells around central eosinophilic collagen. What is the diagnosis?

A 60-year-old man has increasing girth, fatigue, peripheral edema, ascites, hepatomegaly, normal LFTs, enlarged right heart, and interstitial pink amorphous deposits between cardiac myocytes. What is the diagnosis?

A 72-year-old man has dyspnea, orthopnea requiring three pillows, BP 180/100, obesity, bilateral pitting edema, hepatosplenomegaly, basal rales, mild cardiomegaly, pleural effusion, and LVH without valve defects. What is the most likely diagnosis?
A 60-year-old woman with a 30-pack-year smoking history and emphysema dies of CHF with no CAD or valvular disease. What is the most likely cause of right ventricular hypertrophy?
What would confirm that a patient had rheumatic fever during childhood?
options: Thickening of the mitral valve and short fused chordae
answer: Thickening of the mitral valve and short fused chordae

question: A 35-year-old woman has repeated blood pressure readings around 150/92-152/97 after having normal BP at age 18. What change may have occurred?
options: Increased wall thickness of the right ventricle
Volume-mediated dilation of the right ventricle
Increased wall thickness of the left ventricle
Volume-mediated dilation of the left ventricle
answer: Increased wall thickness of the left ventricle

question: A 78-year-old man with recurrent syncope undergoes surgery for a hard, markedly deformed aortic valve and dies during surgery. What is the diagnosis?

A heart tissue sample from a patient with dilated cardiomyopathy is shown. What is the most likely substance seen?

Which type of cells is the target of the initial pathogenesis of atheromatous plaque?
A woman is given scopolamine to prevent motion sickness on a cruise. This agent works through which pathway?
A 32-year-old pregnant woman at 28 weeks has BP 155/95 without proteinuria or preeclampsia signs. Which medication is safe and appropriate?
A patient is taking an ACE inhibitor. Which statement best describes a beneficial effect of ACE inhibitors?
A 38-year-old pregnant woman at 40 weeks needs cervical ripening before labor induction. Which agent is most appropriate?
Which antihistamine has the least antimuscarinic effect?
A woman with familial combined hyperlipidemia starts low-fat diet and cholestyramine. Which compound is reduced the most?
A man has a 5-month dry cough after MI and medications. Which medication most likely caused it?
Which cholinergic drug has the shortest duration?

A patient with prostatic hyperplasia is taking tamsulosin. Which adverse effect can occur?
A man with MI develops ventricular fibrillation and receives IV epinephrine during CPR. Which receptor action explains the rationale?
options: Beta-1 receptor-mediated increase in cardiac contractility
Beta-1 receptor-mediated increase in renin secretion

question: A woman with myasthenia gravis likely overdosed on neostigmine. Which finding is likely?
A child has atropine poisoning with hyperthermia. Which molecular action most likely contributed to hyperthermia?
options: Blockade of prostaglandin synthesis in the hypothalamus
Uncoupling oxidative phosphorylation in skeletal muscle
Blockade of muscarinic receptors in the hypothalamus
Blockade of muscarinic receptors of sweat glands
answer: Blockade of muscarinic receptors of sweat glands

question: A heavy smoker taking oral nicotine and clonidine stops clonidine and develops pounding headache. What is the explanation?
A man has shortness of breath, orthopnea, and bilateral basal crepitations. Which medication is most appropriate to relieve symptoms?
In patients with osteoarthritis taking COX inhibitors, what compound is produced in response to increased COX enzyme activity?
Which vasodilator is produced by endothelial cells?
Dicyclomine primarily exerts its effects by acting on which receptor?
A patient with open-angle glaucoma receives carbachol drops before canaloplasty. What is the primary site of action?
Which drug is known to increase HDL levels?
Drug X changes BP/HR from 120/80, 73 bpm to 140/90, 62 bpm. Which receptor is activated?
A 17-year-old has fever, new heart murmur, migratory polyarthritis, untreated sore throat 3 weeks ago, creatinine 14 U/mL, and ASO 80. What is the next step?
options: Confirm rheumatic fever based on clinical findings only
Suspect rheumatic fever and perform echocardiography
Rule out rheumatic fever due to low ASO titer
Strong evidence against rheumatic fever; do more investigation
answer: Suspect rheumatic fever and perform echocardiography

question: A patient with carditis and joint pain had sore throat one month ago. ASOT was ordered. Which laboratory statement is true?
options: If the titer is high it confirms the diagnosis
If the titer is low it excludes the diagnosis
High titer may indicate previous streptococcal infection
answer: High titer may indicate previous streptococcal infection

question: A patient has fever, subcutaneous nodules, polyarthritis, and a Streptococcus pyogenes sore throat one month ago. What should be in the differential diagnosis?

A man has enlarged right heart, ascites, edema, hepatomegaly, and interstitial pink amorphous deposits between cardiac myocytes. Diagnosis?
A woman has fever, fatigue, weight loss, petechiae, clubbing, mental status changes, massive stroke, and mitral valve lesion at autopsy. Diagnosis?
A 72-year-old woman dies of CHF; aortic valve shows stenosis. What is the most likely cause?
A man 7 days after transmural MI develops hypotension, JVD, muffled heart sounds, and pulsus paradoxus. What process produced these findings?
options: Acute MR due to papillary muscle rupture
Blood accumulation in pericardium due to ventricular wall rupture
answer: Blood accumulation in pericardium due to ventricular wall rupture

question: A patient has chest discomfort and a left atrial mass with the shown pathology. Diagnosis?

answer: Thickening of the mitral valve and short fused chordae

Volume-mediated dilation of the right ventricle

options: Blood in pericardium due to myocardial wall rupture
Acute inflammation due to immune reaction

answer: Blood in pericardium due to myocardial wall rupture
